description: Fix 'Failed to load trips: Failed to fetch' error by adding Next.js API rewrites and updating the API client to use relative paths. The current api-client.ts hardcodes 'http://localhost:3001' as the backend URL, but client-side fetch calls from the browser fail when the backend is not accessible at that address (production, different port, CORS). The fix adds Next.js rewrites in next.config.mjs to proxy /api/* requests to the backend, and updates api-client.ts to use relative '/api' paths. Also fix the broken Home page test.
steps: Update packages/web/next.config.mjs to add an async rewrites() function that maps '/api/:path*' to the backend URL (from NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL env var or default 'http://localhost:3001/:path*'). This makes the Next.js server proxy API calls to the backend, eliminating CORS and connectivity issues.

Update packages/web/src/lib/api-client.ts: Change BASE_URL from 'process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL || http://localhost:3001' to '/api' (relative path). All fetch calls will now go through the Next.js proxy rewrite. This ensures it works in both development and production.
Create packages/web/.env.local with NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=http://localhost:3001 for local development reference (used by next.config.mjs rewrites).
Create packages/web/.env.example documenting the NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL variable for other developers.

Fix packages/web/src/app/__tests__/page.test.tsx: The test expects the h1 to contain 'TripFlow' but the actual h1 content is 'Plan Smarter. Travel Better.' - update the assertion to match the actual heading text.
Run 'pnpm run build' to verify the build passes with the changes.
Run 'pnpm --filter @tripflow/web run test' to verify all web tests pass.
acceptance_criteria: packages/web/next.config.mjs contains a rewrites() function that proxies /api/* to the backend
packages/web/src/lib/api-client.ts uses '/api' as the BASE_URL instead of a hardcoded http://localhost:3001
The frontend fetch calls for trips go to /api/trips (relative) which gets rewritten to http://localhost:3001/trips
pnpm run build succeeds with no errors
pnpm --filter @tripflow/web run test passes all tests including the fixed page.test.tsx
verification: Run: eval "$(mise activate bash)" && pnpm run build
Run: eval "$(mise activate bash)" && pnpm --filter @tripflow/web run test
findings: Build and all 33 web tests pass. .gitignore had .env* pattern that excluded .env.example - added !.env.example exception. .env.local is correctly ignored by git (local dev config). Review feedback addressed: renamed NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL to API_URL, added trailing-slash stripping, added SSR constraint comment.
